location.office_time_required: optional access to office space in Irvine, CA

level_signals: Leads cross-functional roadmap and vendor management independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of Program Management experience, specifically within Fintech, Risk, Financial Operations, or Payments.
A deep understanding of APIs, SQL, and the mechanics of Machine Learning decisioning engines. You understand how risk and operations systems interact at a technical level.
The ability to articulate complex trade-offs to stakeholders, and you have a "builder" mindset capable of leading agile teams.
Expertise in data analysis and the ability to articulate using data the tradeoff between enhancing risk postures against adding friction to the customer experience.
A mindset that prioritizes both the Acorns customer and our internal operations team, with the ability to build a risk platform that reflects our brand.
Exceptional drive and precision in delivery.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission.
Optimistic about the potential of societal change.

scope.surface: Trade and Risk Platform — trading operations, risk workflow orchestration, vendor management, and AI workflow strategies
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer experience quality in trading flows
Risk posture robustness and dynamic rule management
Operational efficiency of Risk Operations teams
core_responsibilities: Collaborate with Trading Operations, Risk, and Data Science to define and execute the trade, risk, and operations roadmap
Improve on the day to day trading experience and lead improvements for the product experience directly related to risk and trading
Build and manage tools that empower Risk Operations teams to update rules and manage queues independently
Lead the evaluation and implementation of third-party trading, risk and operations tools
Drive AI workflow strategies and tools that help scale the existing team's value and efficiency

green_flags: Equity (stock options) and comprehensive benefits included
Clear product surface and ownership area (Trade and Risk Platform)
Company explicitly states candidates are not expected to have all listed requirements
Named one of Time's 'World's Best Brands of 2024' — signal of brand stability
red_flags: E-Verify participation strongly implies no visa sponsorship despite no explicit 'no sponsorship' statement in body — work_auth set to no-sponsorship as inferred
Cultural fit bullets ('A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission') may signal high expectations around personal identity alignment with company mission
Location pay differential noted — compensation may be lower for candidates outside high-cost markets

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated explicitly; inferred from responsibilities)
location.work_auth (inferred as no-sponsorship from E-Verify participation language; JD does not explicitly state 'no sponsorship')
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'market location' language in comp section)
ambiguities: Work authorization: JD mentions E-Verify participation but does not explicitly say 'no visa sponsorship.' Treated as no-sponsorship based on E-Verify language convention, but this is an inference.

nice_to_haves vs must_haves: 'Hunger to deliver game-changing products,' 'A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission,' and 'Optimistic about the potential of societal change' appear in an undifferentiated bullet list alongside harder requirements. Classified as soft/nice-to-have as they read as cultural/motivational rather than verifiable skills.
